articles.title: How a War Affects the UK's Money and Prices
articles.summary: A war between the US, Israel, and Iran is making things harder for the UK's economy. A group called the OECD says the UK's growth, which is how much the country's money grows, will be only 0.7% this year, down from 1.2%. This means the UK is hurt the most among big countries. The war has caused oil and gas prices to go up because ships can't use an important route called the Strait of Hormuz and some plants are damaged. This makes petrol and heating more expensive for people in the UK. Also, inflation, which is how fast prices rise, is predicted to be 4% this year, higher than before. The UK government says they have a plan to protect families, but others worry the economy is weak. The war might also make food prices go up next year if fertilizer stays costly.

articles.title: Social Media Apps Found to Be Addictive in Court
articles.summary: A court in Los Angeles has made a big decision about social media apps like Instagram and YouTube. The jury said these apps are addictive and were made that way on purpose. The companies that own them, Meta and Google, have to pay $6 million to a young woman named Kaley because she got sick from using the apps. She felt sad and had bad thoughts about her body. The companies say they will appeal, which means they want to change the decision. Experts think this ruling is very important and could change how social media works in the future. Other apps like TikTok and Snapchat settled their cases before going to court. This shows that tech companies are worried about these lawsuits.

articles.title: A Big Walk in England Gets a Makeover
articles.summary: In England, there is a special long walk called the Coast to Coast. It goes from the Irish Sea to the North Sea, covering 190 miles through beautiful parks. People start by dipping their boots in the sea at St Bees. The walk was created by Alfred Wainwright over 50 years ago because he wanted a shorter, two-week adventure. Recently, it got a £5.5 million upgrade to make it better. Now, it is an official National Trail with improved paths, signs, and access. This means more people can enjoy the hike safely. The walk is tough but fun, with mountains and valleys. It has history and culture, and markers honor Wainwright. This upgrade helps protect the trail for everyone.

articles.title: Iran Says No to Peace Plan and Asks for Its Own Terms in War
articles.summary: The war between Iran and the United States is still going on after four weeks. Today, Iran said no to a peace plan from the U.S. that wanted Iran to stop its nuclear program, limit missiles, and end a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a busy waterway. In return, the U.S. offered to lift some sanctions. Iran has its own demands, like keeping control of the strait and getting money for the war, which the U.S. probably won't accept. Airstrikes are happening across the Middle East, and talks are continuing, but the White House did not confirm all details of the U.S. plan. The war has no end in sight, and both sides are not agreeing yet.

articles.title: Big Tech Companies Found Responsible for Hurting Kids Online
articles.summary: In two big court cases, juries said that YouTube and Meta (which owns Instagram and Facebook) did something wrong. They made their apps and websites too addictive for children and teens, which can hurt their mental health. The companies knew this could be bad but still designed their platforms to keep kids hooked. In California, they have to pay $6 million, and in New Mexico, Meta must pay $375 million for hiding problems with child safety. A tech expert, Jacob Ward, explained that this is important because it shows the companies' designs, not just what people post, are causing harm. Parents are speaking out, saying they want to protect kids from these addictive features. This is a big step in holding tech companies accountable for how they affect young people.

articles.title: The Government Shutdown and Homeland Security
articles.summary: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has been shut down for 40 days, causing problems for workers and travelers. DHS includes agencies like TSA, which checks people at airports, and workers are missing paychecks. In Washington D.C., leaders from different political parties are trying to make a deal to give money to DHS, but they disagree on some parts. Democrats want changes to how ICE, an agency that enforces immigration laws, works, while Republicans do not want big changes. Senators are meeting quietly to find a solution, and there is hope because many want to fix this. The shutdown affects many people, including TSA workers who face long lines and no pay.
